items.title: Govt assists all states regardless of political affiliation, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 18 — The government does not discriminate against any state in allocating funds, including for agriculture and food security, Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said. He said assistance to all states, including those administered by the opposition, would continue under the leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for the benefit of the people. Mohamad said he would continue seeking additional allocations from the Finance Ministry for 2026 to strengthen Johor’s agriculture sector.

items.title: Japan's extra budget to include funding from fresh debt, source says
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's government will likely issue fresh debt as part of funding for a planned extra budget to cushion the economic blow from the Middle East war, a government source with direct knowledge of the deliberation told Reuters on Monday. Any additional debt issuance would further strain Japan's already worsening finances and may accelerate rises in long-term interest rates. Japan's government is looking at compiling a supplementary budget to ease household energy costs, as fuel prices rise due to the oil shock triggered by the Iran war, sources told Reuters last week.

items.title: MOH, AGC to refine legal aspects of nicotine gel exemption review
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 18 — The Health Ministry (MOH) will hold further discussions with the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) in the near future to refine the legal aspects of the judicial review involving procedural issues related to the exemption of liquid nicotine and nicotine gel from the Poisons List under the Poisons Act 1952. MOH said the court’s decision reflected a healthy system of checks and balances and demonstrated that the public, civil society organisations and other stakeholders were deeply concerned about policies aimed at protecting public health. MOH said it remained committed to strengthening tobacco and smoking product control efforts through continuous monitoring under the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852), particularly to protect children and young people.

items.title: Johor speaker denies knowledge of assembly dissolution, says MB must confirm special sitting
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 18 — Johor State Legislative Assembly Speaker Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi yesterday said he had not been informed about the alleged special session of the state assembly to dissolve the state assembly on June 24 as reported by the media. Speculation about the special state assembly session linked to the dissolution of the state assembly arose after an invitation to all Barisan Nasional (BN) state assemblymen regarding the session on that date went viral. Yesterday, Onn Hafiz, who is also the Johor BN chairman, announced via a post on Facebook that BN would contest all 56 state assembly seats in the upcoming state election.

items.title: PKR confirms Rafizi and Nik Nazmi’s exit, to vacate parliamentary seats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) has confirmed receiving the intention of Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad to vacate their respective parliamentary seats and leave the party. PKR secretary-general Dr Fuziah Salleh said that based on recordings of speeches by the two former senior PKR leaders and following their declaration, Rafizi and Nik Nazmi had ceased to be party members under Clause 9(1)© of the PKR Constitution. According to her, PKR would also examine the membership status of party MPs who attended Rafizi and Nik Nazmi’s announcement ceremony yesterday.

items.title: Kelana Jaya LRT Line disrupted after train fault causes delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Light Rail Transit (LRT) service for the Kelana Jaya Line was delayed this morning following a technical fault involving a train. Rapid KL said the disruption was caused by a train component failure which rendered it unable to move. Following the disruption, Rapid KL operations activated alternative train services and shuttle buses to assist passengers.

items.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone barrage kills four in Russia
items.summary: KYIV, (Ukraine): A wave of almost 600 Ukrainian drones attacked Russia overnight killing four people, authorities said on Sunday, as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called the barrage an "entirely justified" retribution for Moscow's own pummelling of Ukraine. Air defences shot down 556 drones overnight across the country, Russia's defence ministry said, with another 30 drones intercepted after dawn in one of the largest Ukrainian barrages of the ongoing conflict so far. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv's attack on Russia — days after a massive drone and missile bombardment of the Ukrainian capital killed at least 24 people — was "entirely justified."

items.title: Samsung Electronics and its South Korean union resume pay talks as strike risks loom
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics and its South Korean labour union are set to join a new round of government-mediated pay talks on Monday, in a bid to avert a strike at the tech giant, which accounts for nearly a quarter of the country's exports. South Korean President Lee Jae Myung said in a social media post on Monday that corporate management rights should be respected as much as labour rights in the country's free-market economy. South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok said on Sunday the government would pursue all options, including emergency arbitration, to prevent a strike.

items.title: Zizie Izette arrives at court for RM2.8 million corruption hearing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Actress and entrepreneur Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad arrived at the Sessions Court here today for the hearing of her RM2.8 million corruption case. https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/01/1357837/bung-moktar-discharged-corruption-charges-widows-representation Bung Moktar had faced three corruption charges linked to Felcra's approval to invest RM150 million in Public Mutual unit trust funds. The prosecution rejected Zizie Izette's request to withdraw three charges of abetting her late husband.

items.title: Ringgit opens slightly higher against US dollar amid cautious market sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened slightly higher against the US dollar on Monday although cautious sentiment persisted amid escalating geopolitical tensions in Middle East. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said market sentiment remained cautious as the prolonged stalemate between the US and Iran continued to raise concerns over a wider conflict in the region. The ringgit also traded higher against its regional peers.

items.title: #NSTviral: From luxury to research: Billionaire Gabe Newell repurposes superyacht
items.summary: Now renamed Draak, the vessel has undergone extensive modifications, replacing leisure features such as pools and spas with specialised equipment including decompression chambers and heavy-duty cranes, a user on the Reddit forum Bolehland wrote. The conversion is aimed at supporting deep-sea exploration and research operations, read the post. "Following the overhaul, Draak has begun crucial sea trials," read the post.

items.title: Singapore's April exports up 24.5pct, higher than expected
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore's non-oil domestic exports rose by 24.5 per cent in April from a year earlier, government data showed on Monday, led by exports of electronics such as integrated circuits, disk media products and PCs on robust AI-related demand. Among key markets, exports to the US, China and South Korea rose, while shipments to Indonesia were lower than a year earlier, Enterprise Singapore said. Last week, Singapore's acting Minister for Transport Jeffrey Siow said the nation needed to make "bold bets" in new areas such as quantum technologies and space, building on adjacent sectors such as semiconductors, advanced manufacturing and aerospace.

items.title: Rai wins first major at PGA with back-nine birdie blitz
items.summary: NEWTOWN SQUARE, US: Aaron Rai won his first major title on Sunday by capturing the PGA Championship, making birdies on four of the last eight holes to become the first Englishman since 1919 to win the event. Rai sealed a tense back-nine shootout with a spectacular 68-foot birdie putt on the par-three 17th, firing his career-low major round with a five-under-par 65 to finish 72 holes on nine-under 271 at formidable Aronimink. Schmid, seeking his first PGA Tour title in his fifth major start and first PGA Championship, sank a birdie putt from just inside nine feet at 13 to pull within one of the lead.

items.title: Zizie Izette's corruption trial among key court cases this week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The continuation of Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad's corruption trial is among the key court proceedings taking place this week. Zizie Izette, the wife of the late Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, is accused of abetting her husband in obtaining bribes amounting to RM2.8 million linked to Felcra Bhd's investment in Public Mutual Bhd unit trust products. The charges involve alleged bribes of RM2.2 million and RM262,500 from Public Mutual investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid, as well as RM337,500 from Public Mutual unit trust agent Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar.

items.title: Asia shares slip, oil prices pile pressure on bonds
items.summary: Brent was trading up 1.2 per cent at US$110.63 a barrel, while US crude climbed 1.0 per cent to US$106.42 a barrel. Yields on US 10-year notes were up at 4.584 per cent, having surged 23 basis points last week, while 30-year bonds stood at 5.109 per cent after jumping 18 basis points on the week. Japan's Nikkei 225 eased 0.4 per cent, having fallen 2 per cent last week though that was from record highs.

items.title: DBKL leads KL traffic crackdown, issues 211 summonses in tourist hotspots
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A multi-agency enforcement operation led by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) issued 211 traffic summonses and towed 10 vehicles during a crackdown in Jalan Sultan, Jalan Pudu and Bukit Bintang. During the operation, DBKL issued 55 compound notices and towed 10 vehicles for traffic obstruction offences, including parking on public roads and in prohibited areas, under the Road Traffic Rules 1959 and the Road Transport Act 1987. Police issued 21 compound notices, while RTD issued 135 notices against motorists for various traffic offences, obstruction, failure to obey traffic signals and other offences under their respective laws and regulations.

items.title: Oil touches two-week high after drone attack on UAE nuclear power plant
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices extended gains on Monday as efforts to end the US-Israeli war on Iran appeared to have stalled, after a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates came under attack and as US President Donald Trump is expected to discuss military options on Iran. Brent crude futures climbed US$1.44, or 1.32 per cent, to US$110.70 a barrel by 2337 GMT after touching the highest since May 5 earlier in the session. US West Texas Intermediate was at US$107.26 a barrel, up US$1.84, or 1.75 per cent, following a rise to its highest level since May 4.

items.title: PN open to electoral pacts ahead of next general election
items.summary: Its secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the coalition is also open to welcoming strategic partners without requiring them to become component parties. Following the decision, Takiyuddin said PN will establish a special committee comprising representatives from its component parties to refine the necessary terms and conditions. Meanwhile, Takiyuddin said the same meeting decided that the previous decision to accept MIC as a PN component party is no longer relevant and is nullified.

items.title: G7 finance chiefs seek to tackle imbalances as trade strains unity
items.summary: PARIS: G7 finance ministers gathering in Paris on Monday will try to find common ground on tackling global economic tensions and coordinating critical raw material supplies, even as geopolitical differences threaten to test the group's cohesion. The two-day meeting follows a summit between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing that yielded few concrete economic breakthroughs, as tensions over Taiwan and trade simmered beneath a display of diplomatic cordiality. At the core of the Paris agenda will be what French Finance Minister Roland Lescure described as deep-seated global economic imbalances that are fuelling trade friction and risk a turbulent unwinding in financial markets.

items.title: Man wanted over stabbing of woman in car in Kedah arrested
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A 32-year-old man wanted for stabbing a woman inside a Mazda 3 in front of a Kepala Batas restaurant has been arrested. Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali confirmed the arrest and said the suspect would be remanded today. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Southeast Asia suited for smaller narrowbody jets, AirBorneo could benefit: Experts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Smaller narrowbody aircraft such as the Airbus A220 and Embraer E2 family could open new opportunities for airlines in Southeast Asia, particularly on thinner and underserved routes. Endau Analytics aviation analyst Shukor Yusof said Southeast Asia's geography made the region suitable for smaller jet aircraft. An aviation industry expert said smaller narrowbody aircraft could be useful for airlines looking to match aircraft capacity more closely with thinner routes.

items.title: AI scams raise pressure on banks, says Visa
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The rapid rise of artificial intelligence (AI)-driven scams is putting growing pressure on banks and payment firms to upgrade their systems and strengthen fraud detection as digital transactions continue to expand across the region. Visa head of value-added services for regional Southeast Asia Varun Mahindru said trust has become one of the most valuable assets in the digital economy as fraudsters increasingly use AI tools to create more convincing scams. Citing Sumsub's Identity Fraud Report published in November 2025, Mahindru said deepfake-enabled scams in Malaysia surged 408 per cent year-on-year, reflecting the growing sophistication of fraud attempts in the region.

items.title: Malaysia to gain more ground in medical tourism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's medical tourism industry is expected to grow stronger in 2026 as rising healthcare costs and global uncertainty push more international patients to seek affordable and trusted treatment options in Southeast Asia. But Malaysia has built a strong reputation for comparatively lower treatment costs, internationally accredited hospitals, English-speaking healthcare professionals and Muslim-friendly services. STABLE AND TRUSTED DESTINATION Malaysia Healthcare Travel Council (MHTC) said the country continues to be viewed as a stable, neutral and trusted healthcare destination despite broader geopolitical uncertainty affecting global travel patterns.

items.title: Malaysia's economic reset: Restoring income, reviving demand
items.summary: Malaysia must urgently reset its economic direction by confronting two pressures increasingly weighing upon ordinary households-the relentless rise in the cost of living and the steady erosion of purchasing power. Many Malaysians feel trapped in an economic reality where wages struggle to keep pace with escalating prices of food, transportation and essential services. Increasing disposable income is therefore the true engine of sustainable economic growth.

items.title: Cypark: From pioneer to sustainable energy powerhouse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Once a scrappy renewable energy start-up that learned through hard-won experience, Cypark Resources Bhd has emerged as a disciplined, EPCC force with a RM3 billion order book to make Malaysia's circular economy a reality. In an email interview with Business Times, Ami laid out how Cypark has evolved from Malaysia's pioneer in renewable energy deployment into a leading sustainable energy and circular economy expert and why the company's best years are still ahead. School of Hard Knocks Cypark's story is not one of overnight success.

items.title: Hydropolitics, mega-dams and the future of strategic power
items.summary: For much of modern history, water was treated primarily as a developmental issue. Water is no longer merely an environmental or engineering concern. In the emerging era, power increasingly flows through control over systems, networks and critical flows — supply chains, semiconductors, maritime chokepoints, digital infrastructure and increasingly, water itself.

items.title: WHO keeps evaluation of hantavirus as 'low risk'
items.summary: GENEVA: The World Health Organisation said Sunday it was maintaining its assessment of the hantavirus outbreak as "low risk" as the cruise ship where the outbreak originated approached the Netherlands. "The public health risk has been reassessed with the most current information available, and the global risk remains low," said a WHO statement. "While additional cases may still occur among passengers and crew members exposed before containment measures were implemented, the risk of onward transmission is expected to be reduced following disembarkation and the implementation of control measures," it added.

items.title: War in Middle East: Latest developments
items.summary: The war began Feb 28, when US and Israeli forces launched major strikes on the Islamic republic, but peace negotiations have stalled since the April 8 ceasefire and sporadic attacks continue. - Israeli strikes in Lebanon - An Israeli missile strike on an apartment in eastern Lebanon Sunday killed an Islamic Jihad commander and his 17-year-old daughter, Lebanese state media said. Meanwhile, Israeli strikes on towns in southern Lebanon killed five people, including two children, and left at least 15 people wounded, the Lebanese health ministry said.

items.title: Spanish PM faces historic loss in key Andalusia vote
items.summary: SEVILLE: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialists appeared set for an unprecedented drubbing in Andalusian regional elections that could be a dress rehearsal for next year's national vote, partial results showed. Regions wield wide-ranging powers in areas including health, education and housing in Spain's decentralised political system, making the election in the country's most populous region especially significant. The main conservative Popular Party (PP), which has governed the southern region since 2019, was on course to win 52 seats, leaving it three short of another majority in the historic Socialist heartland.

items.title: LCS missile setback exposes deeper defence risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Imagine spending time assembling a jigsaw puzzle, only to realise one final important piece is missing. For Malaysia's littoral combat ship (LCS) programme, Norway's decision involving the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system presents a similar challenge. The bigger story may not even be about the missile system itself.

items.title: Rain Rave knife case sparks debate: Can offenders with mental disorders face charges in court?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 18 — The recent knife-wielding incident at the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in Bukit Bintang has raised questions surrounding the treatment of individuals with mental disorders within the Malaysian legal system. Here’s a look at how Malaysian law interprets mental health in relation to criminal liability and the potential consequences for crimes committed by individuals found to have mental disorders. According to lawyer Rajsurian Pillai, Malaysian law does not automatically exempt individuals with mental disorders from criminal liability.

items.title: Trump issues dire warning to Iran to accept peace deal
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump issued a fresh warning to Iran on Sunday, saying it had to move quickly towards a peace deal or "there won't be anything left of them." Lebanon's health ministry said new Israeli strikes Sunday on the country's south killed five people, including two children. On Sunday, Iranian media said the United States had failed to make any concrete concessions in its latest response to Iran's proposed agenda for negotiations to end the war.

items.title: Michelin-bib apam in hands of 3rd generation
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: For nearly four hours each day, 65-year-old R. Neelamegan endures the intense charcoal-fire heat as he carries on his family's more than a century old apam manis business. Using charcoal fire and claypots imported from India, the traditional cooking method gives the apam a distinctive aroma. Neelamegan, better known as Ravi among customers, said his father, N. Rajagopal, came from Tamil Nadu, India.

items.title: Nearly 60pc of screened workers overweight
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 60 per cent of Malaysian workers who underwent health screenings were found to be overweight or obese, a figure a deputy minister described as worrying. Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the 59.2 per cent figure was very high and could not be taken lightly. Also present were Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy and international) and acting secretary-general Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar, Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail, Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (operations) Sutekno Ahmad Belon, Perkeso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal and Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.

items.title: Matrix Concepts unveils lifestyle hub in Kluang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has launched X Park Bandar Seri Impian, a recreational and lifestyle destination in Kluang, Johor. Matrix Concepts said the launch marks the second X Park township collaboration after Bandar Sri Sendayan, positioning Bandar Seri Impian as another hub for recreational activities. Matrix Concepts chairman Datuk Mohamad Haslah Mohamad Amin said X Park Bandar Seri Impian is more than a recreational facility; it serves as a community hub that brings people together through shared activities and experiences.

items.title: TNB is using AI like a ‘smart GPS’ for electricity — and it could transform Malaysia’s energy grid
items.summary: TNB chief digital and information officer Azlan Ahmad said AI for energy is essentially about giving the power grid “a brain and eyes” capable of predicting, optimising and protecting the system in real time. Azlan said AI now functions like “a weather forecaster for electricity”, helping TNB anticipate changes in electricity demand and prepare the system earlier. Beyond efficiency, he said, AI is also helping utilities strengthen grid reliability and safety.

items.title: Muslims in Malaysia to observe Hari Raya Aidiladha on May 27
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Muslims in Malaysia will celebrate Hari Raya Aidiladha on Wednesday, May 27, corresponding to 10 Zulhijjah 1447 Hijrah. The announcement was made by Keeper of the Rulers’ Seal assistant secretary Muhammad Affendi Jalaludin during a ‘live’ broadcast on national television tonight. “Accordingly, Hari Raya Aidiladha for all states throughout Malaysia is fixed on Wednesday, May 27, 2026,” he said.

items.title: ‘Kamikaze worth taking’: What we know so far about Rafizi and Nik Nazmi’s new party Bersama
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 17 — Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today formally unveiled their new political platform, Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), confirming they are quitting PKR and vacating their parliamentary seats in Pandan and Setiawangsa. Parti Bersama Malaysia is a Penang-based political party founded by Tan Gin Theam and officially registered on December 2, 2016. Rafizi said efforts to register a completely new political party were expected to fail.

items.title: ‘I am not following’: Anwar stays tight-lipped on Rafizi and Nik Nazmi’s move to new party Bersama
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he is not following the latest political developments involving two former senior party leaders, Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad. He said this when asked to comment on the decision of the former deputy president and former vice resident of PKR on reportedly taking over a political party known as Parti Bersama Malaysia or Malaysian United Party (MUP). MUP is a political party registered on December 2, 2016 in Penang.

items.title: PM Anwar says Putrajaya considering higher RON95 subsidy quota for delivery riders
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government plans to increase the Budi Madani RON95 (BUDI95) subsidised petrol quota limit for p-hailing delivery riders. The prime minister said the proposal is for the current 200-litre limit to be increased to 250 litres for riders. “Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil will also be tasked with deliberating the proposal in an effort to increase an additional 50 litres from the original quota.

items.title: Putrajaya focused on improving BUDI95, not redefining T20 group, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, May 17 — The government has not discussed any proposal to introduce a new classification for the top 20 per cent income group (T20), Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said. He said the government is instead focused on improving the existing BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) initiative, which he described as comprehensive and fair to all groups. Commenting on the National Union of the Teaching Profession’s (NUTP) call for the government to reconsider the provision of 300 litres of monthly fuel subsidy under BUDI95 for teachers, Amir Hamzah said the government must take into account global oil market conditions and the subsidy burden it bears.

items.title: Anwar says he wants good faith talks with Zahid after Johor BN election stance
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim intends to meet his counterpart in Barisan Nasional (BN) Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi to discuss the coalition’s Johor leadership decision to contest on its own in the upcoming state election. “I hope to meet the BN chairman, who is also the Umno president, to discuss in detail (the current situation in Johor). On a related matter, Anwar called on Taman Desa Mutiara PPR residents to support the PH candidate in the coming state election.

items.title: Civil servant arrested in Terengganu after police find pistols, suspected drugs and handcuffs in MPV
items.summary: KUALA BERANG, May 17 — A civil servant was arrested after police discovered firearms and suspected drugs in his possession during a patrol operation conducted by the Hulu Terengganu District Police Headquarters on May 10. He said inspections uncovered two pistols, two kerambit blades, a pair of handcuffs and several transparent packets believed to contain drugs. The investigation also involves Section 8 of the Arms Act 1960, Section 6 of the Corrosive and Explosive Substances and Offensive Weapons Act 1958, Section 89 of the Police Act 1967 and Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, he said.

items.title: Loke says MPs elected on party tickets should serve full term after Rafizi, Nik Nazmi quit PKR
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Those elected on party tickets should serve till the end of their term to respect the people’s mandate, Pakatan Harapan (PH) deputy chairman Anthony Loke. Loke was commenting on the actions of former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former PKR vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, who announced their decision to quit their party and to vacate their parliamentary seats effective tomorrow. Rafizi, the Pandan MP and Nik Nazmi, the Setiawangsa MP will reportedly hand in their letters regarding their seats to the Dewan Rakyat Speaker tomorrow and their PKR resignation letters to PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh on Tuesday.

items.title: Rafizi says new party Bersama to go solo in next polls, vows no coalition deals
items.summary: Speaking at a press conference here today following the announcement of Bersama’s takeover of an existing party structure, Rafizi said the movement would adopt a multiracial, open-platform approach and field candidates across constituencies currently held by both government and opposition blocs. We are multiracial and we can contest anywhere,” he said. Rafizi said the party’s long-term objective is to build a political movement centred on ideas and generational renewal, drawing parallels with the early years of PKR and DAP.

items.title: Kota Laksamana finally gets its own RM6.5m Japerun Complex
items.summary: MELAKA, May 17 — The Kota Laksamana state constituency will soon have its own State Legislative Assembly Development and Coordination Committee Complex (Japerun). Kota Laksamana assemblyman Low Chee Leong said the complex will be built on a 0.83-hectare site in Kota Laksamana Jaya, featuring offices and a multipurpose hall. Kota Laksamana has had no proper Japerun building.

items.title: Kedah school bus driver turns to funeral transport work after losing students, vehicle seized by JPJ
items.summary: BALING, May 17 — A 73-year-old school bus driver in Kedah was forced to turn his vehicle into a paid passenger transport service after struggling to find students to ferry over the past two years, leading to the bus being seized by the Road Transport Department (JPJ) for allegedly misusing its permit. Kedah JPJ director Stien Van Lutam said investigations found that the bus had not been transporting schoolchildren for the past two years due to a lack of demand. Although the vehicle had valid insurance coverage and road tax, Van Lutam said it had breached the conditions of its operator’s licence by carrying passengers other than school students.

items.title: Amir Hamzah to seek audience with Pahang Sultan this week over federal allocation concerns
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, May 17 — Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan will seek an audience with the Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah in the near future to clarify issues regarding the Federal Government’s allocation to the state of Pahang. Amir Hamzah said the Federal Government’s commitment is clear in ensuring that every allocation to the state is made based on needs and the Federal Constitution. On May 8, the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, while officiating the Opening Ceremony of the First Meeting of the Fifth Term of the 15th Pahang State Legislative Assembly, questioned the Federal Government’s allocations to the state.

items.title: Anwar signals early polls if unity government pressures persist
items.summary: Anwar also said the ruling coalition would not hesitate to hold a snap general election if pressure mounted following Johor Barisan Nasional’s (BN) and Umno’s decision yesterday. Anwar said PH’s relationship with BN remained good. Yesterday, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that Johor BN would contest all 56 state constituencies in the upcoming state election.

items.title: Zambry: Higher education access must align with national policy, unity goals, stresses BM and History requirements for UEC holders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — The government is adopting a balanced, inclusive and responsible approach in its efforts to expand access to national higher education as part of building a common meeting point for Malaysia’s diverse society. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the commitment must nevertheless remain aligned with efforts to uphold the foundations of the National Education Policy and the National Education Philosophy. “It also involves nation-building, strengthening unity, empowering Bahasa Melayu as the national language, as well as understanding Malaysia’s history and nationhood.

items.title: ‘Silk Road’ Scholarship offers full sponsorship for Malaysian undergraduates, postgraduates in China
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 17 — Malaysian students can now apply for the Chinese Government’s fully funded “Silk Road” Scholarship Programme for the 2026 intake, offered by Kibing Group in partnership with Changsha University of Science and Technology (CSUST) and supported by Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS). The scholarship will fully sponsor five undergraduate students and five to 10 master’s students in fields related to new energy and engineering. The undergraduate programme in New Energy Science and Engineering will be taught in Mandarin, while the master’s programme in Power Engineering and Engineering Thermophysics is offered in Mandarin or English.

items.title: Talk to your own BN minister: Loke tells Wee Ka Siong where to bring UEC grouses
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has told MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong to direct his complaints regarding the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) to his own coalition partner, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. Speaking at the 2026 Pakatan Harapan Convention today, Loke pointed out that since Zambry is from Barisan Nasional (BN) and MCA is a component party, any dissatisfaction should be resolved internally within their coalition. So, don’t blame Anthony Loke and DAP.

items.title: Gagasan Rakyat amends constitution, aligns divisions with 26 parliamentary seats, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 17 — Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah (Gagasan Rakyat) is fully prepared to face the 16th General Election (GE16) following a constitutional amendment to restructure its divisions according to the 26 parliamentary constituencies in Sabah and Labuan. Its president Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor said the move would strengthen the party’s grassroots machinery and streamline preparations ahead of the parliamentary polls. “We are ready to face the coming election, organise our party strategy and strengthen the machinery based on parliamentary constituencies.

items.title: Now, Loke wants Pakatan to contest all 36 seats in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: Loke declared that PH should contest all 36 state seats in Negeri Sembilan, in direct retaliation to Johor BN’s announcement yesterday that it would contest all 56 seats in the upcoming state election. “We will also contest all 56 state seats in Johor, and I suggest that PH in Negeri Sembilan do the same and contest all 36 state seats,” he said. “If the Johor state assembly is dissolved tomorrow, we will also dissolve the Negeri Sembilan state assembly tomorrow,” he declared.

items.title: Labuan Corp lodges police report over viral online casino allegation tied to Pulau Papan
items.summary: LABUAN, May 17 — Labuan Corporation (LC) has lodged a police report calling for a thorough investigation into a viral Facebook post that allegedly tarnished its image by linking it to a proposed casino development on Pulau Papan. LC chief executive officer Rithuan Ismail said the report was lodged at the Labuan police station on May 15 following the posting, which had used the photographs of him and LC deputy chairman Simsudin Sidek. Rithuan said Pulau Papan, one of Labuan’s well-known island attractions, has strong potential in sustainable tourism, recreational activities, nature-based products, heritage elements and family-oriented attractions.

items.title: Cabinet greenlights RM10b rapid transit project for JB, Loke confirms
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The federal government has approved the long-awaited rapid transit project aimed at alleviating chronic traffic congestion here, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. “The Cabinet has just approved the RM10 billion project. Loke added that the transit project is crucial to support the upcoming Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, which is scheduled to begin operations in 2027.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: JS‑SEZ proof of Madani govt’s economic strategy success, accounts for 66pc of Johor investments
items.summary: Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the unique project, resulting from cooperation between the two countries, has successfully attracted major investments, accounting for 66 per cent of Johor’s total investments over the past year. “We are replacing outdated border management systems, expediting procurement programmes, building new systems, and putting in place the most advanced infrastructure. He said this in his speech at the PH Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.

items.title: Thunderstorms to lash large swathes of Malaysia until late evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for states across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak, cautioning residents to brace for heavy rain, strong winds and lightning until 7.00pm. In Peninsular Malaysia, the affected states include Perlis, several districts in Kedah — namely Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena and Padang Terap — as well as districts in Perak, Pahang, Selangor, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor. The warning covers densely populated and economically significant areas, including parts of the Klang Valley such as Gombak, Hulu Selangor and Hulu Langat.

items.title: Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi quit PKR, will vacate Pandan and Setiawangsa seats
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 17 — Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said they will vacate the Pandan and Setiawangsa seats tomorrow. The duo said they would notify the Dewan Rakyat Speaker of their withdrawal tomorrow. Rafizi and Nik Nazmi also said they would formally write to the PKR secretary-general on Tuesday to inform the party of their decision.

items.title: Johor BN, Pakatan contesting all seats won’t affect federal cooperation, says Fahmi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The decision by Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) in Johor to each contest all 56 seats in the next state election will not affect cooperation at the federal government level, said PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said the stance expressed by Johor BN and PH was made at the state level and does not impact the existing cooperative relationship. Yesterday, Johor Menteri Besar and state BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the decision to contest all state seats, followed by Johor PH chief Aminolhuda Hassan, who made a similar announcement when speaking at the convention today.
